title: RefinementList widget shows dups when case type differs b/w facet and record
body: **Describe the bug 🐛** The RefinementList widget shows duplicate listings in edge cases where the case type of a selected facet differs from the case type of the related record item (e.g. TitleCase vs UPPERCASE). The bug appears when the facet is selected outside the normal InstantSearch flow (e.g. manually via the helper instead of directly in the refinementList widget). **To Reproduce 🔍** 1. Using the default "basic routing" template, select `Samsung`: https://lyw7mov3ql.csb.app/?refinementList%5Bbrand%5D%5B0%5D=Samsung 2. Mimic a manual facet selection by changing the url from TitleCase `Samsung` to UPPERCASE `SAMSUNG` https://lyw7mov3ql.csb.app/?refinementList%5Bbrand%5D%5B0%5D=SAMSUNG 3. Refresh the page 4. Observe the correct results appear, but the refinementList shows duplicates (one for each case type) **Expected behavior 💭** RefinementList should only show one listing. **Screenshots 🖥** **Environment:** All **Additional context** Most definitely an edge case. Everything works as expected if the facet is selected via the refinementList widget itself. It's only when the facet is changed manually elsewhere AND the records contain multiple case types that the bug appears. I believe this stems from the Algolia engine using the case type of the first record indexed when defining each facet. So if record 1 shows TitleCase `key: Value` and record 2 shows UPPERCASE `key: VALUE`, the facet will be recorded as TitleCase for all records containing any version of `key: value`. In my scenario, visitors can refine facets by clicking directly on the results item (see screenshot below). InstantSearch produces the correct results, but now the UPPERCASE version of the facet starts polluting the client producing the unintended results. The upstream fix for most folks is to ensure records are normalized prior to indexing, but in my scenario the records need to be presented as-is. ![Screen Shot 2019-07-30 at 9 28 09 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/314653/62138293-09804b80-b2ad-11e9-915f-7f5e90f3b120.png)
